title: Sphaerostephanos elatus

The classification of this plant places it in the kingdom Plantae, phylum Pteridophyta, and class Polypodiopsida. Further classification includes it in the order Polypodiales and the family Dryniaceae. intro: Sphaerostephanos elatus is a species of fern in the family Dryniaceae, commonly known as the Elated Shield Fern. The Elated Shield Fern has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the Elated Shield Fern is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the form of a paste or ointment to treat skin conditions and wounds.
